title: Rich Rested Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A light and flavorful Moroccan dish featuring tender lamb and a unique cheese-pull texture, made with gelatine leaves and rested to perfection.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Soak the gelatine leaves in cold water for 5 minutes, then drain and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chicken broth, dried apricots, and soaked gelatine leaves, and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer to the preheated oven, where it will cook for 2.5 hours.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it rest for 30 minutes before serving.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
